methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $178M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Heron Power is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Drew Baglino that has raised $178M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Lanzatech, ticker LNZA) operating in the same sector at similar or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $178M in total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $338M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector comparables at Series C stage: KoBold Metals ($3.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M). The median valuation across this set is $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $3.0B.

These methods triangulate to a low of $287M, median of $669M, and high of $1.1B. The $382M spread between low and high reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate discount to Series C peers and the appropriate multiple on capital raised. The peer-median method produces an outlier high relative to stage-progression, suggesting that either Heron is differentiated relative to the KoBold/Electric Hydrogen/Amogy cohort, or those comparables command premium valuations not yet justified by Heron's current development stage. Resolution of this disparity—through revenue traction, technology risk reduction, or revised peer selection—would narrow the range materially.
